name: the-new-jim-crow
description: Michelle Alexander's 'The New Jim Crow: Mass Incarceration in the Age of Colorblindness' — a landmark analysis of how mass incarceration functions as a new racial caste system. Alexander argues that the War on Drugs systematically targets Black communities, labels millions as felons, and then subjects them to legalized discrimination in voting, employment, housing, and education — locking them into a permanent second-class status.
useCase: Understanding how mass incarceration functions as a racial caste system, the War on Drugs as the mechanism, the role of racialized drug enforcement, the collateral consequences of a felony conviction, and what advocates are doing to dismantle the New Jim Crow.
knownRisks: Controversial thesis — some scholars dispute the comparison to Jim Crow
Critics argue the analysis overlooks class factors and violent crime
The book is polemical advocacy, not an impartial analysis
